title: foeniculum vulgare var. subinodorum
intro: Foeniculum vulgare var. subinodorum, also known as wild or Italian fennel, is a variety of the common fennel plant that is characterized by its lack of flowering stalks. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Foeniculum vulgare var. subinodorum include its ability to reduce flatulence, alleviate menstrual cramps, and support immune function. Fennel preparations, such as tea, tincture, and essential oil, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
